model_narrative: We plan production of a set of items over a sequence of time periods on shared resources. Items are linked by a bill of materials, so producing one item creates internal demand for the components that go into it, on top of the external customer demand for finished items. In each period we decide which items to produce and how many units of each to make. The objective is to minimize a weighted total of production that pushes output toward later periods, with quantities produced earlier in the horizon penalized more heavily than the same quantities produced later.

sets.name: PP
sets.doc: the set of stock-keeping units (SKUs / items) that can be produced; some items are end products with external demand and others are components used to build other items

sets.name: TT
sets.doc: the planning periods (time buckets) in chronological order; the first member is the earliest period and each later member follows the one before it

sets.name: KK
sets.doc: the set of shared production resources whose capacity is consumed when items are produced

params.name: R
params.doc: the number of units of the first item required to build one unit of the second item, defining the bill of materials; zero when the first item is not a component of the second

params.name: demand
params.doc: the external (independent) customer demand for an item in a period, in units; zero when there is no external demand

params.name: LT
params.doc: the production lead time of an item, given as a whole number of periods; production started in a period only becomes available to satisfy requirements that many periods later

params.name: LS
params.doc: the minimum lot size of an item, in units; whenever an item is produced in a period the quantity produced must be at least this amount

params.name: I
params.doc: the beginning on-hand inventory of an item available at the start of the horizon, in units

params.name: U
params.doc: the fraction of a resource's available capacity consumed by producing one unit of an item; the resource capacity in each period is normalized to one

params.name: M
params.doc: a large per-item constant used to switch the production-quantity upper bound on or off depending on whether the item is produced in that period; large enough never to restrict a genuine production decision

vars.name: d
vars.doc: production indicator; equals 1 if the item is produced in the period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of the item produced in the period, in units

vars.name: objvar
vars.doc: the accounting variable holding the objective value

constraints.name: TT)
constraints.intent: Set the objective accounting variable equal to a weighted total of everything produced across all items and all periods, where each unit of production is weighted by how early in the horizon it is made. Production in the earliest period carries the largest weight and the weight decreases by one for each later period, so that the last period carries a weight of one. The accounting variable must equal the sum of these weighted production quantities over every item and every period.

constraints.name: TT)
constraints.intent: For each item and each period, everything available to meet that item's needs through the end of that period must be at least everything required of it through that period. What is available is the beginning inventory of the item plus all of its production that has had time to become usable, meaning production started early enough that its lead time has already elapsed by this period. What is required is the cumulative external demand for the item together with the cumulative internal demand created by producing the items that use it as a component, both accumulated from the start of the horizon through this period.

constraints.name: x[p,
constraints.intent: For each item and each period, whenever the item is produced the quantity made must be at least its minimum lot size, and when the item is not produced no production quantity is forced. The amount produced must be at least the lot size scaled by whether the item is produced in that period.

constraints.name: x[p,
constraints.intent: For each item and each period, no production can take place unless the item is marked as produced in that period. The quantity produced must not exceed the item's large allowable ceiling scaled by whether the item is produced, so that the quantity is forced to zero whenever the item is not produced and is otherwise effectively unrestricted by this rule.

constraints.name: U[p,
constraints.intent: For each period and each resource, the total amount of that resource consumed by all production scheduled in that period must not exceed the resource's available capacity, where each unit produced of an item consumes its share of the resource and the available capacity in a period is one.

query: Only two AJ8172 runs in the whole plan feels risky for a finished good — one hiccup and we're short. Couldn't we spread it over at least three separate builds? Make it run three AJ8172 setups and show me why the optimizer didn't want to.
expected_pyomo: model.aj_three_runs = Constraint(expr=sum(model.d['AJ8172', t] for t in model.TT) >= 3)
